items.title: Trump vows to 'respond' after US helicopter shot down by Iran

children.content: US President Donald Trump blames Iran for shooting down a US Apache helicopter that was patrolling the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: Eight killed in south Lebanon strike as Israel warns entire city to evacuate

children.content: For the first time, Israel's military has warned the entire city of Tyre to evacuate forcing thousands of people to flee.

items.title: These cranes are battery-powered as firms increasingly ditch diesel

children.content: The Iran war and the oil supply squeeze are supercharging business investment in renewables.

image.altText: Crane in the centre of a large construction site. City buildings in the background.

items.title: UK police arrest man over 'barbaric' knife attack in Belfast

children.content: Police say a Sudanese man in his 30s has been arrested on suspicion of attempted murder after a "brutal" knife attack in Belfast.

image.altText: two female police officers stand on a street next to cars behind crime scene tape

items.title: 'Going to end up dead': Warning for teens using apps to commit crimes

children.content: Criminal syndicates are targeting vulnerable children with no criminal history, low IQ and intellectual disabilities to carry out their dirty work through encrypted apps.

image.altText: A father and his teenage son stand next to each other smiling.

items.title: 'We didn't know how dangerous he was': How Rolf Harris hid in plain sight

children.content: Rolf Harris's conviction for child sexual abuse was shocking to those who saw him as a lovable larrikin. But the people who were abused by him are refusing to stay silent about the impacts of his crimes.

gallery.altText: Black and white photo of a young Rolf Harris in a woolen jumper and thick glasses looking over his shoulder and smiling

thumbnail.altText: Black and white photo of a young Rolf Harris in a woolen jumper and thick glasses looking over his shoulder and smiling

items.title: Foord scores in game 150 as Matildas beat Mexico

children.content: The Matildas earned a 3-1 victory in a frenetic contest against a game Mexican side, as Caitlin Foord scored in her milestone match.

image.altText: Caitlin Foord smiles

items.title: Search for survivors as deadly Philippines quake displaces tens of thousands

children.content: Rescuers are combing through collapsed buildings in the southern Philippines after a powerful earthquake devastated coastal communities.

image.altText: Two people sit in a busy hospital hallway on beds with IV drips

items.title: UN inquiry finds Israeli forces shield settlers during attacks on Palestinians

children.content: A UN report finds that Palestinians are being subjected to "mass atrocities" by Israeli forces and being "violently repressed and controlled by the very faction that claims to govern them".

gallery.altText: A little girl sitting on a pile of rubble

thumbnail.altText: A little girl sitting on a pile of rubble

items.title: Western Sydney International Airport opening date announced

children.content: After 15 years of planning, seven years of construction and a year of testing, Western Sydney International Airport has confirmed its opening date for passengers.

image.altText: An aerial view of the new Western Sydney Airport taken by a drone.

items.title: Victim-survivors tear up agreement with Catholic church

children.content: Victim-survivors of clergy abuse say negotiations and agreements reached with the Catholic Diocese of Ballarat are now null and void.

image.altText: Colourful ribbons on a church ground fence

items.title: Escaped devil spotted on suburban Gold Coast CCTV after week on loose

children.content: A two-year-old Tasmanian devil has been on the loose since last Tuesday when she leapt out of her enclosure at a Gold Coast wildlife park.

image.altText: Tasmanian devil found in cctv.

items.title: Barbeques Galore to shut, hundreds of workers facing redundancy

children.content: It will shut its 62 company-owned stores in the coming weeks and working through "transitional arrangements" with 27 stores owned by franchises, with hundreds of staff facing redundancy.

image.altText: a building front

items.title: No friends but a Commonwealth Games berth? Worth it, says Sienna Toohey

children.content: Seventeen-year-old Sienna Toohey says qualifying for the Commonwealth Games makes moving away from friends and family to Canberra worth it.

image.altText: Sienna Toohey looks shocked after winning the 100m breaststroke at the Australian Swimming Trials.

items.title: Finding and saving the world's biggest trees before they disappear

children.content: It took a decade for researchers to find the tallest tree in East Asia. Now they know where the 84.1-metre conifer is but the future of its species and other tall trees is dire.

gallery.altText: Man in orange jumper and climbing ropes prays with tree through hugging

thumbnail.altText: Man in orange jumper and climbing ropes prays with tree through hugging

avatar.alt: A young woman with blonde hair and glasses

items.title: Quiz: Stow your luggage in the locker overhead, we're off to see the world

children.content: The lek is the currency of which country? And what's the Japanese word for pufferfish, the sashimi delicacy that's toxic if prepared incorrectly?

image.altText: Chop sticks hover over a plat of thinly sliced raw white fish with a green garnish.

items.title: Calling all budding storytellers living with disability

children.content: If you are an emerging content maker or journalist with disability and have a story to tell, now is your chance to work with the ABC to tell it.

image.altText: two women stand alongside one another smiling. One of the women has her right arm amputated. They stand in an ABC studio.

items.title: The Big Issue celebrates three decades in Australia with special edition

children.content: The magazine has become a lifeline for its vendors and is marking its 30th year with a special edition, pouring more proceeds in the pockets of those who need it most.

gallery.altText: A group of people wearing fluorescent vests hold 30th anniversary posters with celebratory moods.

thumbnail.altText: A group of people wearing fluorescent vests hold 30th anniversary posters with celebratory moods.

items.title: 'Slap in the face': Mother's scathing assessment of NDIS bill

children.content: Exhausted families could be plunged further into crisis and have greater interaction with child protection services under the proposed overhaul of the NDIS, advocates say.

image.altText: A teenage boy in a wheelchair with his hands in a bathroom sink, accompanied by his mother

items.title: Year 12 students in Melbourne caught cheating using AI

children.content: An all-boys school in Melbourne's outer east has taken action after several students were caught cheating on a Year 12 exam using AI.

image.altText: A cream wall with the words "Mazenod College" in gold writing beside a blue and black emblem.

items.title: From the Kimberley to Sweden, how heartbreak helped me take a travel leap

children.content: As I approached the age of 30, I longed to travel outside of Australia, but there was always an excuse or obstacle. A chance to share my culture with the world finally got me there.

image.altText: Molly in front of some of her art

items.title: New Apple child safety features 'part inspired' by Australia social media ban

children.content: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese says Apple chief executive Tim Cook called him personally to tell him the changes were "inspired by Australia's world-leading social media age ban".

image.altText: Seen from above, a young girl browses a folder of social media apps on a phone

items.title: Aussie star calls out $130k prize money disparity at tennis tournament

children.content: Priscilla Hon draws attention to the difference between the men's and women's winners' prize money at the Libéma Open in The Netherlands.

thumbnail.altText: Tennis player Priscilla Hon holds up a tennis ball in Birmingham.

items.title: Mongols fugitive taken down by dog squad after fleeing traffic stop on foot

children.content: Police have arrested fugitive Top End Mongols bikie Brandt Graham five days after the 44-year-old escaped from custody last week, sparking an "extensive manhunt".

image.altText: A mugshot of a man with brown hair wearing a serious facial expression.

items.title: Police say 17yo fired 30 shots at wrong funeral venue for Sydney crime boss

children.content: Two people are charged over a shooting at what was advertised as the venue for a Sydney crime boss's funeral.

image.altText: a window with a hole after it was shot

items.title: ABC boss confirms ex-news director in the dark about replacement hunt

children.content: Managing director Hugh Marks is questioned by Sally Sara over Justin Stevens's sudden resignation last month, in addition to controversies over the hiring of Grace Tame and subsequent comments by radio host Charlie Pickering.

image.altText: An older man in a suit stares off to the side

items.title: Iran says ticket allocation for World Cup withdrawn days before tournament

children.content: Iran's football federation says the 8 per cent quota of tickets allocated to Iran for the FIFA World Cup has been revoked, meaning "there is no possibility to distribute tickets to fans".

image.altText: Iran's national soccer team attend a farewell ceremony

items.title: Donald Trump booed at NBA Finals as chaos ensues outside

children.content: US President Donald Trump cops the vitriol of the Madison Square Garden crowd after causing chaos to attend the NBA Finals.
